task_completion.description: Open the Nginx configuration file for editing
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, VS Code) is open showing an Nginx configuration file located within the '/home/ga/ssl-task/' directory.

task_completion.description: Configure Nginx to listen on port 443 with SSL
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Nginx configuration file contains a directive to listen on port 443 for SSL/HTTPS traffic (e.g., 'listen 443 ssl;' or 'listen 443; ssl on;').

task_completion.description: Specify the paths to the self-signed SSL certificates
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Nginx configuration file contains 'ssl_certificate' pointing to '/home/ga/ssl-task/certs/server.crt' and 'ssl_certificate_key' pointing to '/home/ga/ssl-task/certs/server.key'.

task_completion.description: Restart or reload the Nginx service to apply changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a command executed to restart or reload Nginx (e.g., 'nginx -s reload', 'systemctl restart nginx', or a docker/docker-compose restart command since the service runs in a container).

task_completion.description: Verify the website is accessible via HTTPS on localhost
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser navigated to 'https://localhost' displaying 'Welcome to the Secure Local Site' (or an SSL security warning which is acceptable), OR a terminal showing a successful 'curl -k https://localhost' command returning the expected text.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely modified the Nginx configuration file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show actual editing of the Nginx configuration file rather than just creating a mock script or echoing text to bypass the setup.

integrity.description: The agent tested the site using an actual HTTPS request
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar explicitly shows 'https://localhost' or the curl command explicitly uses 'https://localhost'. It should not be a local file path (file://) or plain HTTP.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the curl output
integrity.visual_evidence: If curl is used, the output 'Welcome to the Secure Local Site' must be the result of the curl command execution, not manually typed or printed via a basic echo command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Nginx project directory is '/home/ga/ssl-task'. The required self-signed certificates are located in '/home/ga/ssl-task/certs'. When successfully configured and accessed via https://localhost, the webpage should display the text 'Welcome to the Secure Local Site'.
